externalAuth.title: External Auth
externalAuth.description: Manage OAuth2 / OIDC providers used for site account login. Test the connection before exposing it to users.
externalAuth.emptyDescription: Add an OIDC identity source so users can sign in with external accounts.
externalAuth.keyHint: Generated when saved. Used by login entry and redirect URI paths, and cannot be changed manually.
externalAuth.iconUrlHint: Optional. Use an HTTPS URL or a same-origin path starting with /. Empty or failed images fall back to the default icon for this provider type.
externalAuth.enabledDesc: Disabled providers disappear from the login page. Existing account links are not deleted.
externalAuth.secretHint: Leave blank if the provider does not require a secret.
externalAuth.secretKeepPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current secret
externalAuth.secretKeepHint: A secret is configured. Saving an empty value keeps the current secret.
externalAuth.testHint: The test reads discovery metadata or validates manual endpoints.
externalAuth.testPartial: Connection test completed, {{failed}} of {{total}} checks failed
externalAuth.deleteDescription: Users will no longer be able to start new logins with this provider. Local accounts are not deleted.

kindDesc.generic_oauth2: Manually configure authorization, token, and userinfo endpoints for non-standard OAuth2 services.
kindDesc.github: Use a GitHub OAuth App and read the verified primary email from the user emails API.
kindDesc.google: Use Google OpenID Connect with fixed issuer and standard email verification semantics.
kindDesc.microsoft: Use Microsoft or Entra ID sign-in with issuer handling controlled by the Microsoft provider.
kindDesc.oidc: Reads authorization endpoints from issuer discovery metadata. Best for standard OIDC services.
kindDesc.qq: Use QQ Connect OAuth2 with fixed token, openid, and userinfo endpoints.

github.description: GitHub uses fixed OAuth endpoints and default scopes. Enter only the OAuth App Client ID and Client Secret here.

google.description: Google uses a fixed OpenID Connect issuer. The frontend does not allow issuer or manual endpoint overrides.

microsoft.description: Microsoft derives the issuer from fixed identity platform rules. Do not configure custom authorization, token, or userinfo endpoints here.

qq.description: QQ uses fixed OAuth2, openid, userinfo endpoints, and default scopes. Enter only the QQ Connect app credentials here.

type.title: Choose type
type.description: Pick OIDC, generic OAuth2, or a dedicated platform based on the identity source.

connection.title: Connection
connection.description: Enter client credentials and endpoints, then test before saving.

review.title: Review
review.description: Decide whether this provider should be available immediately.

dialog.description: These settings affect the login page and account linking flow.
dialog.connectionDesc: OIDC uses an issuer URL. Generic OAuth2 uses manual endpoints. GitHub, Google, Microsoft, and QQ use fixed connection parameters.
